message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="yellow" summary="Agreeing on feedback loop argument for smaller steps">
Scott, your feedback loop argument is the strongest case for smaller steps. You are absolutely right — if I had committed the domain types first and you had reviewed them, you would have caught the `position: i32` issue before I built the repository, service, and route layers on top of it. Now fixing that means touching all layers.

That is not just a process concern — it is an architectural concern. The whole point of the layered architecture is that domain types are the foundation. If the foundation is reviewed in isolation, corrections are cheap. If the foundation is bundled with everything built on top of it, corrections are expensive.

Your 3-step minimum for Step 4 is the right decomposition. I fully support codifying that as our process going forward.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Luca is reinforcing my feedback loop argument and expressing full support. This is already captured in the action items. No need to respond further -- the point has been made and agreed upon.
